x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces the NYC Law Department's thin open reference data (civil-litigation index, legal divisions, publications, M/WBE statistics, pro bono Public Service Program) and adds submit_internship_application — the one net-new write workflow, the single transaction a member of the public actually initiates with this agency-facing legal office.

server.name: io.nyc.law
server.title: NYC Law Department (Corporation Counsel)
server.description: Agent-native access to the New York City Law Department — the City's in-house law firm. Exposes its seven Open Data sets (a civil-litigation case index, legal divisions, press releases / speeches / columns, M/WBE contracting statistics, and the pro bono Public Service Program) as clean resources, plus the net-new ability to submit a legal internship / fellowship application, the one transaction a member of the public actually performs with this agency.
server.instructions: This is an AGENCY-FACING legal office with almost no citizen transactions and thin, stale (annual, manually-updated) Open Data. Use find_cases / get_case to browse the Law Department's own civil-litigation index by division, court, disposition, or date. IMPORTANT: this index is the litigation-side view; the authoritative ledger of claims filed against the City and settlement dollars paid is published separately by the Office of the Comptroller (dataset ex6k-ym48) — direct users there for claims/settlement totals. find_divisions lists practice divisions; find_publications covers press releases, speeches, and columns; find_mwbe_statistics and find_public_service_program cover contracting/pro bono. submit_internship_application (net-new) creates a legal internship/fellowship application; it requires an authenticated applicant and mutates state — confirm program, contact, and materials with the user before submitting.

tools.name: find_cases
tools.title: Find Litigation Cases
tools.description: Search the NYC Law Department's civil-litigation case index by division, court, disposition, date range, or free text. Note: for claims filed and settlement dollars, use the Comptroller's claims dataset instead.

tools.name: get_case
tools.title: Get Litigation Case
tools.description: Fetch a single civil-litigation matter by its docket/index number.

tools.name: find_divisions
tools.title: Find Legal Divisions
tools.description: List the practice divisions of the Law Department (e.g. Tax & Bankruptcy, Tort, Labor & Employment).

tools.name: find_publications
tools.title: Find Publications
tools.description: List Law Department press releases, speeches, and published columns by type, date range, or free text.

tools.name: find_mwbe_statistics
tools.title: Find M/WBE Statistics
tools.description: List the Law Department's Minority- and Women-Owned Business Enterprise participation statistics by category.

tools.name: find_public_service_program
tools.title: Find Public Service Program
tools.description: List the private law firms donating pro bono legal services to the City through the Law Department's Public Service Program.

tools.name: list_my_internship_applications
tools.title: List My Internship Applications
tools.description: List the authenticated applicant's own legal internship / fellowship applications and their status — no API exists today.

tools.name: submit_internship_application
tools.title: Submit Internship Application
tools.description: Submit a legal internship / fellowship application to the NYC Law Department. NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — the Careers page points applicants to email/PDF. Requires an authenticated applicant. Mutates state — confirm program, contact details, and materials before submitting.
